Adam James Clarkson

Brewer of design, software & beer

Moving to Jekyll

12 Oct 2013

The last incarnation of my blog didn't last very long on the web. It was an experiment in trying to bend wordpress to my will. I wanted to blog about my experiences with design and development, but have a site that was more expressive than a typical blog allows. There are quite a lot of plugins out there that allow you to "Art Direct" your posts, and tweak the theme based on your needs. They always come with a caveat or two though, and usually involves some strange hack to get everything to behave correctly.

It doesn't have to be that way though. I set about writing posts in markdown and using a Ruby script to automatically generate the navigation to make these files a site. All well and good, but there was an existing product out there doing exactly what I wanted, and doing it well. Enter, Jekyll. #!/home/ajclarkson/.rvm/bin/rvm-shell 1.9.3-p429 GITREPO=$HOME/ajclarkson.co.uk.git TMPGITCLONE=$HOME/tmp/ajclarkson.co.uk.git PUBLICWWW=/srv/www/ajclarkson.co.uk/public

            git clone $GIT_REPO $TMP_GIT_CLONE
            /home/ajclarkson/.rvm/gems/ruby-1.9.3-p429/bin/jekyll build -s $TMP_GIT_CLONE -d $PUBLIC_WWW
            rm -Rf $TMP_GIT_CLONE
            exit</code>
